sources.label: Seeking Alpha - Comfort Systems forecasts mid-to-high 20% 2026 same-store revenue growth, $12.5B backlog (Q1 print, pre-Jun 6)
Yahoo Finance - A Look At Comfort Systems USA (FIX) Valuation After A Strong Multi Month Share Price Run
StockInvest.us - FIX daily price action and trend check (Jun 8-9)
TIKR - Comfort Systems data center demand and record backlog context

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental information since June 6; today's -3.6% dip is flow-driven profit-taking after a 78% YTD run, and on this crowding-sensitive alpha page the modest pullback merely offsets the residual-alpha drag from a 90% Strong Buy consensus and recent insider selling. Rank 99 in the Monitor/benchmark bucket already reflects that FIX sits outside the core passive/power bottleneck stack.
verdicts.bull: Record $12.45B backlog (up 81% YoY) with technology work over half of revenue gives multi-year visibility into data-center mechanical/cooling demand, and a -3.6% pullback with the thesis intact marginally improves entry asymmetry.
verdicts.neutral: Fundamentals are excellent but fully discovered: mid-to-high 20% same-store growth guidance is in consensus, the stock trades at record margins and a stretched multiple, so it functions as an AI-capex barometer rather than an alpha source.
verdicts.bear: 78% YTD on top of a multi-year 20x run, ~$58.5M of insider sales in three months, and unanimous sell-side bullishness mean any data-center capex digestion or margin normalization from record 26.3% gross margins triggers a sharp de-rating.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings/backlog print (late July) confirming or breaking the mid-to-high 20% same-store growth guide.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded post-rerating positioning unwinding on any hyperscaler capex digestion signal or margin mean-reversion from record levels.
